sourceMovie.title: New Gods: Nezha Reborn
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto New Gods: Nezha Reborn (2021) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and fantasy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Raya and the Last Dragon
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If New Gods: Nezha Reborn clicked for you, Raya and the Last Dragon (2021) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and fantasy, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Constantine: City of Demons - The Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If New Gods: Nezha Reborn clicked for you, Constantine: City of Demons - The Movie (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and fantasy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after New Gods: Nezha Reborn?
faq.answer: Try Raya and the Last Dragon, Constantine: City of Demons - The Movie, Ne Zha 2—each shares DNA with New Gods: Nezha Reborn while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is New Gods: Nezha Reborn?
faq.answer: New Gods: Nezha Reborn (2021) is best described as Animation, Fantasy, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is New Gods: Nezha Reborn part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "New Gods Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
